seoTitle: Rixs Creek vs Rob Roy — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rixs Creek and Rob Roy. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Rixs Creek vs Rob Roy: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rixs Creek and Rob Roy.
heading: Rixs Creek vs Rob Roy — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rixs Creek, New South Wales and Rob Roy, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rob Roy has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Rixs Creek (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rixs Creek or Rob Roy safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rixs Creek has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Rob Roy scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rixs Creek scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Rob Roy scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rixs Creek or Rob Roy?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rob Roy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
